---
title: "MA ALAN 4-28-26"
author: "MR"
date: "2026-04-27"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)

# set wd
setwd("~/GitHub_Projects/ALAN_MA_2026/MA spring 2026")

#load packages
library(metafor)
library(tidyverse)
```

```{r load & clean dataframe}

# load in dataframe
df<- read.csv("ALAN Meta-Analysis Class 4-28-2026.csv")

view(df)


# remove unncessary columns and rows
df <- df %>%
  select(1:time_of_assay) |> slice(1:230)

df<- df |> filter(!is.na(yi))
df$vi <- as.numeric(df$vi)
```


```{r check yi and vi}
summary(df$yi)
summary(df$vi)

any(is.na(df$yi))
any(is.na(df$vi))
any(df$vi <= 0)
```

```{r run meta-analysis}

# remove all non LRR effect sizes, any missing effect sizes, and include Study_ID and effect_ID as random effects
df_ma <- df %>% 
  filter(
    eff_size_type == "LRR",
    !is.na(yi),
    !is.na(vi),
    vi > 0
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Study_ID = as.factor(study_number),
    effect_ID = row_number()
  )



# Random-effects / multilevel meta-analysis
res_ma <- rma.mv(
  yi = yi,
  V = vi,
  random = ~ 1 | Study_ID/effect_ID,
  data = df_ma,
  method = "REML"
)

summary(res_ma)


res_mod <- rma.mv(
  yi, vi,
  mods = ~ spectra_bin + lux_treatment + behavioral_category + experiment_duration_hr,
  random = ~ 1 | Study_ID/effect_ID,
  data = df_mr
)

summary(res_mod)
summary(res_mod)$beta
```

```{r meta-regression}

library(dplyr)

# bin colors into broad categories
df_ma <- df_ma %>%
  mutate(
    spectra_peak_num = suppressWarnings(as.numeric(spectra_peak_treatment_nm)),
    
    spectra_bin = case_when(
      # white LEDs (text)
      grepl("white", spectra_peak_treatment_nm, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ "white",
      
      # blue: ~400–499 nm
      !is.na(spectra_peak_num) & spectra_peak_num < 500 ~ "blue",
      
      # green: ~500–569 nm
      !is.na(spectra_peak_num) & spectra_peak_num >= 500 & spectra_peak_num < 570 ~ "green",
      
      # red: ~570+ nm
      !is.na(spectra_peak_num) & spectra_peak_num >= 570 ~ "red",
      
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    
    spectra_bin = factor(spectra_bin, levels = c("blue", "green", "red", "white"))
  )



# run MA with spectra binned, lux, behavioral category, and exp duration but no interactions
res_mod <- rma.mv(
  yi, vi,
  mods = ~ spectra_bin + lux_treatment + behavioral_category + experiment_duration_hr,
  random = ~ 1 | Study_ID/effect_ID,
  data = df_mr
)


summary(res_mod)
summary(res_mod)$beta
```

```{r spectra x behavior + duration x behavior}
res_mod2 <- rma.mv(
  yi, vi,
  mods = ~ spectra_bin * behavioral_category +
          lux_treatment +
          behavioral_category* experiment_duration_hr,
  random = ~ 1 | Study_ID/effect_ID,
  data = df_ma
)

summary(res_mod2)
summary(res_mod2)$beta
```

```{r main forest plot}


# Main random-effects model
res_main <- rma.mv(
  yi, vi,
  random = ~ 1 | Study_ID/effect_ID,
  data = df_ma,
  method = "REML"
)
summary(res_main)
summary(res_main)$beta

# create ordering index

df_ma_ord <- df_ma %>%
  arrange(yi)   # increasing: most negative to most positive

res_main_ord <- rma.mv(
  yi, vi,
  random = ~ 1 | Study_ID/effect_ID,
  data = df_ma_ord,
  method = "REML"
)

forest( # PLOT OF ALL EFFECT SIZES NOT GROUPED
  res_main_ord,
  slab = df_ma_ord$Study_ID,
  transf = exp,
  refline = 1,
  xlab = "Response ratio"
)


# Aggregate within each study
study_es <- df_ma %>%
  group_by(Study_ID) %>%
  do({
    res <- rma(yi, vi, data = ., method = "REML")
    data.frame(
      yi = as.numeric(res$b),
      vi = res$se^2
    )
  }) %>%
  ungroup()

# Overall study-level model
res_study <- rma(
  yi, vi,
  data = study_es,
  method = "REML"
)

# Add weights
study_es <- study_es %>%
  mutate(
    weight = weights(res_study),
    weight_perc = 100 * weight / sum(weight),
    weight_label = paste0(round(weight_perc, 1), "%")
  )

# Forest plot with study name and weight
png("forest_plot.png", width = 1400, height = 900, res = 300)

forest(
  res_study,
  slab = study_es$Study_ID,
  transf = exp,
  refline = 1,
  xlab = "Response ratio",
  header = c("Study", "Response ratio [95% CI]"),
  ilab = study_es$weight_label,
  ilab.xpos = -0.5,
  order = order(study_es$yi),
  cex = 0.7
)

text(
  x = -0.5,
  y = length(study_es$yi) + 2,
  labels = "Weight",
  font = 2,
  cex = 0.7
)

dev.off()
```


```{r main caterpillar plot}


# Extract study-level random effects
re <- ranef(res_main)$Study_ID

# Convert to dataframe
df_re <- data.frame(
  Study_ID = rownames(re),
  yi = re[,1],
  ci_lb = re[,2],
  ci_ub = re[,3]
)

# Convert to response ratio
df_plot <- df_ma %>%
  mutate(
    RR = exp(yi),
    RR_lb = exp(yi - 1.96 * sqrt(vi)),
    RR_ub = exp(yi + 1.96 * sqrt(vi))
  ) %>%
  arrange(RR) %>%
  mutate(id = row_number())

ggplot(df_plot, aes(x = RR, y = id, color = Study_ID)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = RR_lb, xmax = RR_ub), height = 0.2) +
  geom_point(aes(size = 1 / (RR_ub - RR_lb))) +
  scale_size(range = c(1, 3)) +
  scale_x_log10() +
  labs(
    x = "Response ratio",
    y = "Effect size",
    color = "Study"
  ) +
  theme_classic()
```


```{r}

df_plot <- df_ma %>%
  filter(!is.na(yi), !is.na(vi), vi > 0) %>%
  mutate(
    RR = exp(yi),
    RR_lb = exp(yi - 1.96 * sqrt(vi)),
    RR_ub = exp(yi + 1.96 * sqrt(vi))
  )

ggplot(df_ma, aes(x = spectra_bin, y = exp(yi), color = Study_ID)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.15, alpha = 0.6, size = 2) +
  
  stat_summary(
    aes(group = spectra_bin),
    fun = mean,
    geom = "point",
    color = "black",
    size = 3
  ) +
  
  stat_summary(
    aes(group = spectra_bin),
    fun.data = mean_cl_normal,   # <-- adds 95% CI
    geom = "errorbar",
    width = 0.2,
    color = "black"
  ) +
  
  facet_wrap(~ behavioral_category) +
  scale_y_log10() +
  labs(
    x = "Spectral category",
    y = "Observed response ratio",
    color = "Study"
  ) +
  theme_classic()
```

```{r}
ma_mod <- rma.mv(
  yi, vi,
  mods = ~ spectra_bin * behavioral_category +
          lux_treatment +
          behavioral_category* experiment_duration_hr,
  random = ~ 1 | Study_ID/effect_ID,
  method = "ML",
  data = df_ma
)

summary(ma_mod)
```


```{r}
ma_test <- rma.mv(
  yi, vi,
  mods = ~ spectra_bin * behavioral_category * lux_treatment * experiment_duration_hr,
  random = ~ 1 | Study_ID/effect_ID,
  data = df_ma
)

summary(ma_test)

anova(res_main_ml, ma_test)
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

# Extract coefficients
coefs <- as.data.frame(summary(ma_mod)$beta)
coefs$term <- rownames(coefs)

# Get SE and CI
vcov_mat <- vcov(ma_mod)
se <- sqrt(diag(vcov_mat))

coefs <- coefs %>%
  mutate(
    se = se,
    ci_lb = V1 - 1.96 * se,
    ci_ub = V1 + 1.96 * se,
    RR = exp(V1),
    RR_lb = exp(ci_lb),
    RR_ub = exp(ci_ub)
  )

# Clean names (optional but recommended)
coefs$term <- gsub("spectra_bin", "", coefs$term)
coefs$term <- gsub("behavioral_category", "", coefs$term)

# Plot
ggplot(coefs, aes(x = RR, y = reorder(term, RR))) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = RR_lb, xmax = RR_ub), height = 0.2) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  scale_x_log10() +
  labs(
    x = "Response ratio (model estimate)",
    y = NULL,
    title = "Moderator analysis"
  ) +
  theme_classic()
```

```{r confirm model fit}
res_main_ml <- rma.mv(
  yi, vi,
  random = ~ 1 | Study_ID/effect_ID,
  data = df_ma,
  method = "ML"
)

ma_mod_ml <- rma.mv(
  yi, vi,
  mods = ~ spectra_bin * behavioral_category +
          lux_treatment +
          behavioral_category * experiment_duration_hr,
  random = ~ 1 | Study_ID/effect_ID,
  data = df_ma,
  method = "ML"
)

anova(res_main_ml, ma_mod_ml)

summary(ma_mod_ml)
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

df_ma <- df_ma %>%
  mutate(
    spectra_bin = factor(spectra_bin),
    behavioral_category = factor(behavioral_category)
  )

newdat <- expand.grid(
  experiment_duration_hr = seq(
    min(df_ma$experiment_duration_hr, na.rm = TRUE),
    max(df_ma$experiment_duration_hr, na.rm = TRUE),
    length.out = 100
  ),
  spectra_bin = factor("white", levels = levels(df_ma$spectra_bin)),
  behavioral_category = levels(df_ma$behavioral_category),
  lux_treatment = mean(df_ma$lux_treatment, na.rm = TRUE)
)

newdat$behavioral_category <- factor(
  newdat$behavioral_category,
  levels = levels(df_ma$behavioral_category)
)
# Build model matrix
X <- model.matrix(
  ~ spectra_bin * behavioral_category +
    lux_treatment +
    behavioral_category * experiment_duration_hr,
  data = newdat
)

# Remove intercept
X <- X[, colnames(X) != "(Intercept)", drop = FALSE]

# Get coefficient names from model
coef_names <- rownames(summary(ma_mod)$beta)

# Remove intercept from coefficient names
coef_names <- coef_names[coef_names != "intrcpt"]

# Add any missing model columns to X as 0
missing_cols <- setdiff(coef_names, colnames(X))

for(col in missing_cols) {
  X[, col] <- 0
}

# Remove columns not in the model
X <- X[, coef_names, drop = FALSE]

# Predict
pred <- predict(ma_mod, newmods = X)

# Combine
plotdat <- newdat %>%
  mutate(
    RR = exp(pred$pred),
    RR_lb = exp(pred$ci.lb),
    RR_ub = exp(pred$ci.ub)
  )
```


```{r}
ggplot(plotdat, aes(x = experiment_duration_hr, y = RR, color = behavioral_category)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_ribbon(
    aes(ymin = RR_lb, ymax = RR_ub, fill = behavioral_category),
    alpha = 0.2,
    color = NA
  ) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  scale_y_log10() +
  labs(
    x = "Exposure duration (hours)",
    y = "Predicted response ratio",
    color = "Behavior",
    fill = "Behavior"
  ) +
  theme_classic()
```


```{r behavior category effects}
library(dplyr)
library(metafor)

# keep valid rows
df_tmp <- df_ma %>%
  filter(!is.na(yi), !is.na(vi), vi > 0)

# pool effects within each behavioral category
cat_es <- df_tmp %>%
  group_by(behavioral_category) %>%
  do({
    m <- rma(yi, vi, data = ., method = "REML")
    data.frame(
      yi = as.numeric(m$b),
      vi = m$se^2,
      ci_lb = m$ci.lb,
      ci_ub = m$ci.ub
    )
  }) %>%
  ungroup()

# forest plot (RR scale)
forest(
  cat_es$yi,
  vi = cat_es$vi,
  slab = cat_es$behavioral_category,
  transf = exp,
  refline = 1,
  xlab = "Response ratio",
  header = "Behavioral category"
)
```
